query: What is the maximum capacity threshold for a  generator in MISO to be eligible for the fast-track interconnection process in MISO, according to MISO Tariff Attachment X Generator Interconnection Procedures (GIP)? Cite the relevant source report and section for your answer
final_answer:
I have found the answer in the authoritative source.

## Answer

The maximum capacity threshold for a generator to be eligible for the **Fast Track Process** in MISO is **5 MW (five MW)**.

### Supporting detail

The MISO Tariff, Attachment X – Generator Interconnection Procedures (GIP), Section 1 (Definitions), defines:

> **"Fast Track Process** shall mean the procedure for evaluating an Interconnection Request for a certified **Small Generating Facility no larger than five MW** that includes the screen set forth in Section 14, customer options meeting, and optional supplemental review."

The threshold ties to the definition of a **Small Generating Facility**, also in Section 1:

> **"Small Generating Facility** shall mean a Generating Facility that has an aggregate net Generating Facility Capacity of **no more than five MW** and meets the requirements of Section 14 and Appendix 3."

So the Fast Track Process is available to certified Small Generating Facilities with an aggregate net capacity of **no more than 5 MW**, evaluated using the screening process set forth in **Section 14** of the GIP (with certification criteria in Appendix 3). (Note: a separate, even simpler track—the **10 kW Inverter Process**, per Appendix 4—applies to certified inverter-based facilities no larger than 10 kW.)

### Source
- **MISO FERC Electric Tariff, Attachment X – Generator Interconnection Procedures (GIP), Section 1 (Definitions)** — definitions of "Fast Track Process" and "Small Generating Facility" (Effective January 31, 2025). 
URL: https://cdn.misoenergy.org/Attachment%20X%20-%20GIP%20(Effective%20January%2031%202025)330116.pdf

**Assumption noted:** I used the currently effective version of Attachment X (effective Jan 31, 2025). The 5 MW Fast Track threshold reflects MISO's adoption of FERC Order No. 2023 small generator interconnection reforms, which raised the prior 2 MW Fast Track threshold to 5 MW.
